sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.98 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: Suppose that$G$is a solvable finite group and$A$is a group of automorphisms of$G$of relatively prime order. Is there a bound for the Fitting height$h(G)$of$G$in terms of$A$and$h(C_G(A))$, or even in terms of the length$l(A)$of the longest chain of nested subgroups of$A$and$h(C_G(A))$? When$A$is solvable, it is proved in (A.\,Turull, J.~Algebra, 86 (1984), 555--566) that$h(G) h(C_G(A)) + 2 l(A)$and...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
